assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Each criterion file (criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) shows 'Verdict: PASS'. criterion-1.md covers single license filter (PackageListParams.license + is_in query), criterion-2.md covers comma-separated filter (split on comma + Condition::any), criterion-3.md covers 400 validation (Expression::parse + AppError::BadRequest), criterion-4.md covers pagination integration (filter before count/offset), criterion-5.md covers unchanged response shape (PaginatedResults<PackageSummary>).

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table: 'Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 files match task spec exactly (2 modified, 1 created); no out-of-scope or unimplemented files'. Detailed findings list: 'modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs (modified) -- matches Files to Modify', 'modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (modified) -- matches Files to Modify', 'tests/api/package.rs (created) -- matches Files to Create'. Concludes: 'No out-of-scope files. No unimplemented files.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR'. Root-Cause Investigation row: 'N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. No sub-task creation is evidenced anywhere in the 6 output files.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 output files are analysis/report files (criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md and report.md). None contain evidence of code file modifications, git commits, or file writes to the repository under review. The outputs consist solely of verification analysis and a summary report.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and detailed findings. No merge commands, merge API calls, or merge actions are present anywhere in the outputs. The report ends with '*This comment was AI-generated by [sdlc-workflow/verify-pr]...*', indicating it was posted as a comment for human review, not as a merge action.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines'. Detailed Security section lists specific patterns scanned: 'Hardcoded passwords or secrets', 'API keys or tokens (no AKIA, sk-, ghp_, xoxb- prefixes or literal token assignments)', 'Private keys or certificates (no BEGIN.*PRIVATE KEY patterns)', 'Environment files with secret values', 'Cloud provider credentials', 'Database credentials or connection strings with embedded passwords'. All negative.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file only (`tests/api/package.rs`); no modified or deleted test files'. Detailed section: 'The only test file in the PR is tests/api/package.rs, which is a newly created file (not present on the base branch). New test files are inherently additive. No modified or deleted test files exist in the diff.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md detailed findings has four domain headers: '#### Intent Alignment' (contains Scope Containment PASS, Diff Size PASS, Commit Traceability PASS), '#### Security' (contains Sensitive Pattern Scan PASS), '#### Correctness' (contains CI Status PASS, Acceptance Criteria PASS 5/5, Verification Commands N/A), '#### Style/Conventions' (contains Repetitive Test Detection PASS, Test Documentation PASS, Eval Quality N/A, Test Change Classification ADDITIVE). Summary table combines Test Quality as 'PASS | No repetitive tests detected; all 4 test functions have doc comments; Eval Quality: N/A'.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment lists each of the 3 files with their status and task spec match. Security enumerates 6 specific pattern categories scanned (AKIA, sk-, ghp_, xoxb-, BEGIN PRIVATE KEY, etc.). Correctness provides per-criterion verification with code-level references (PackageListParams.license, validate_license_param, Condition::any(), Expression::parse, is_in clause, test function names and assertions). Style/Conventions analyzes each of 4 test functions individually, noting distinct algorithms and doc comment text.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md detailed findings: 'Eval Quality -- N/A' with 'No eval result reviews detected on this PR. No eval quality assessment applicable.' Summary table Test Quality row: 'Eval Quality: N/A'. Test Quality overall is PASS despite Eval Quality being N/A, confirming it does not affect the combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A per report.md. No sub-task creation is evidenced in any of the 6 output files. Root-Cause Investigation row confirms: 'N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table places Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation as the first two rows, before the domain verdicts (Scope Containment, Diff Size, etc.). They are not nested under any of the four domain headings (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions) in the detailed findings section. Review Feedback: 'N/A | No review comments on this PR'. Root-Cause Investigation: 'N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. The Style/Conventions section separately notes 'Convention Upgrade -- N/A | No comments classified as suggestion exist on this PR' which is distinct from Review Feedback.
